Declarations for ECMA object operations, including creation, deletion, and property definition functions.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that defines function prototypes for operations on ECMA objects, which are part of the ECMAScript language specification. It includes declarations for functions that handle object creation, deletion, and property definition, as well as functions for managing object default values and prototype settings. The file includes necessary headers such as `ecma-conversion.h` and `ecma-globals.h` to support these operations. The header guards `ECMA_OBJECTS_GENERAL_H` prevent multiple inclusions of the file, ensuring that the declarations are only processed once during compilation.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `ecma-conversion.h`
- `ecma-globals.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

### ecma\_reject<!-- {{#callable_declaration:ecma_reject}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/operations/ecma-objects-general.h#L29>)

Returns a type error or a simple false value based on a flag.
- **Description**: Use this function to handle operations that can either throw a type error or return a simple false value. The function checks the provided flag to determine the appropriate action. It is useful in scenarios where conditional error handling is required, such as in operations that may fail under certain conditions. Ensure that the flag is correctly set to achieve the desired behavior.
- **Inputs**:
    - `is_throw`: A boolean flag that determines the function's behavior. If true, the function raises a type error. If false, it returns a simple false value. The caller must provide a valid boolean value.
- **Output**: Returns an `ecma_value_t` which is either a type error or a simple false value, depending on the `is_throw` flag.
- **See Also**: [`ecma_reject`](<ecma-objects-general.c.md#ecma_reject>)  (Implementation)

### ecma\_op\_create\_object\_object\_noarg<!-- {{#callable_declaration:ecma_op_create_object_object_noarg}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/operations/ecma-objects-general.h#L30>)

Creates a new ECMAScript object with the default prototype.
- **Description**: Use this function to create a new ECMAScript object that has the default object prototype. This function does not take any arguments and returns a pointer to the newly created object. It is suitable for cases where an object is needed without any initial properties or custom prototype. Ensure that the returned object is properly managed to avoid memory leaks.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to a newly created `ecma_object_t` with the default prototype.
- **See Also**: [`ecma_op_create_object_object_noarg`](<ecma-objects-general.c.md#ecma_op_create_object_object_noarg>)  (Implementation)

### ecma\_op\_create\_object\_object\_arg<!-- {{#callable_declaration:ecma_op_create_object_object_arg}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/operations/ecma-objects-general.h#L31>)

Creates an object from a given ECMA value.
- **Description**: Use this function to create an object from a specified ECMA value. It converts the input value to an object if it is of type object, number, string, or boolean. If the value is undefined or null, it creates a new object with no arguments. This function is useful when you need to ensure that a value is represented as an object, regardless of its original type. It must be called with a valid ECMA value.
- **Inputs**:
    - `value`: An ECMA value that serves as the argument for the constructor. It can be of type object, number, string, boolean, undefined, or null. The function will convert or create an object based on this value. The caller must ensure the value is a valid ECMA value.
- **Output**: Returns an ECMA value that represents the created object. If the input is an object, number, string, or boolean, it returns the object form of the value. If the input is undefined or null, it returns a new object.
- **See Also**: [`ecma_op_create_object_object_arg`](<ecma-objects-general.c.md#ecma_op_create_object_object_arg>)  (Implementation)

### ecma\_op\_create\_object\_object\_noarg\_and\_set\_prototype<!-- {{#callable_declaration:ecma_op_create_object_object_noarg_and_set_prototype}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/operations/ecma-objects-general.h#L32>)

Creates an object and sets its prototype.
- **Description**: Use this function to create a new object with a specified prototype. It is useful when you need an object that inherits properties from a given prototype. The function can accept a null pointer if no prototype is desired, in which case the object will have no prototype. This function is typically used in environments where objects need to be dynamically created with specific inheritance chains.
- **Inputs**:
    - `object_prototype_p`: Pointer to the prototype object for the new object. Can be null if no prototype is needed. The caller retains ownership of the prototype object.
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to the newly created object with the specified prototype set.
- **See Also**: [`ecma_op_create_object_object_noarg_and_set_prototype`](<ecma-objects-general.c.md#ecma_op_create_object_object_noarg_and_set_prototype>)  (Implementation)

### ecma\_op\_general\_object\_delete<!-- {{#callable_declaration:ecma_op_general_object_delete}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/operations/ecma-objects-general.h#L34>)

Attempts to delete a property from an object.
- **Description**: Use this function to delete a specified property from an object. The function checks if the property exists and whether it is configurable. If the property is configurable, it deletes the property and returns a success value. If the property does not exist, it also returns a success value. If the property is not configurable and the `is_throw` flag is set to true, it raises a type error. Otherwise, it returns a failure value. Ensure that the object is not a lexical environment and that both the object and property name are valid and non-null before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `obj_p`: A pointer to the object from which to delete the property. Must not be null and must not be a lexical environment.
    - `property_name_p`: A pointer to the name of the property to delete. Must not be null.
    - `is_throw`: A boolean flag that determines error handling behavior. If true, a type error is raised when attempting to delete a non-configurable property.
- **Output**: Returns an `ecma_value_t` indicating the result of the operation: success if the property is deleted or does not exist, failure if the property is non-configurable and `is_throw` is false, or a type error if `is_throw` is true and the property is non-configurable.
- **See Also**: [`ecma_op_general_object_delete`](<ecma-objects-general.c.md#ecma_op_general_object_delete>)  (Implementation)

### ecma\_op\_general\_object\_default\_value<!-- {{#callable_declaration:ecma_op_general_object_default_value}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/operations/ecma-objects-general.h#L35>)

Returns the default value of an object based on a type hint.
- **Description**: Use this function to obtain the default value of an object, considering a preferred type hint. The function must be called with a valid object that is not a lexical environment. If the hint is `ECMA_PREFERRED_TYPE_NO`, the function determines the hint based on the object's class name, defaulting to string for Date objects and number otherwise. The function attempts to call `toString` or `valueOf` methods on the object, depending on the hint, and returns the result. If these methods are not callable or return an error, the function raises a type error.
- **Inputs**:
    - `obj_p`: A pointer to an `ecma_object_t` representing the object whose default value is needed. Must not be null and must not represent a lexical environment.
    - `hint`: An `ecma_preferred_type_hint_t` indicating the preferred type of the result. Can be `ECMA_PREFERRED_TYPE_NO`, `ECMA_PREFERRED_TYPE_STRING`, or `ECMA_PREFERRED_TYPE_NUMBER`.
- **Output**: Returns an `ecma_value_t` representing the default value of the object. If an error occurs, it returns an error value.
- **See Also**: [`ecma_op_general_object_default_value`](<ecma-objects-general.c.md#ecma_op_general_object_default_value>)  (Implementation)

### ecma\_op\_general\_object\_define\_own\_property<!-- {{#callable_declaration:ecma_op_general_object_define_own_property}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/operations/ecma-objects-general.h#L36>)

Defines or modifies a property on an object.
- **Description**: Use this function to define a new property or modify an existing property on a given object. The function requires a property descriptor that specifies the attributes of the property, such as configurability, enumerability, writability, and value. The function checks if the object is extensible and if the property can be modified according to the descriptor. If the operation fails and the `is_throw` flag is true, an error is thrown. This function must be called with valid object and property name pointers, and it assumes the object is not a lexical environment.
- **Inputs**:
    - `object_p`: Pointer to the object on which the property is defined. Must not be null and must not point to a lexical environment.
    - `property_name_p`: Pointer to the property name. Must not be null.
    - `property_desc_p`: Pointer to the property descriptor that defines the attributes of the property. Must not be null.
    - `is_throw`: Boolean flag that controls error handling. If true, the function throws an error on failure; otherwise, it returns a simple value indicating failure.
- **Output**: Returns an `ecma_value_t` indicating success or failure of the operation. If `is_throw` is true and the operation fails, an error is thrown.
- **See Also**: [`ecma_op_general_object_define_own_property`](<ecma-objects-general.c.md#ecma_op_general_object_define_own_property>)  (Implementation)
